decisions.summary: Fix lives in the live server pipeline runForkPipeline (conversations.ts:3862), NOT the legacy lib createSummaryForkPromise — the latter has no production callers (only a comment in fork-client.ts:10).

Resilience is a degradation ladder: authored doc → LLM summary (truncation-resilient) → heuristic local summary (generateFallbackSummary, no LLM, cannot overflow) → focus-only empty seed. The pipeline ALWAYS reaches ensureForkSessionReady and spawns.

Fix #1 (truncate-and-retry) is implemented once in the shared generateSummaryFromPrompt (smart-compaction.ts:791) as a head+tail truncate + single retry. This covers all 5 call sites (chunked loops + non-chunked) and makes authorHandoffExternal precompaction resilient too — DRY single chokepoint.

Context-overflow detection extends src/lib/context-overflow.ts (already owns overflow semantics) with isContextOverflowError(err) matching 'prompt is too long' + 'blocking_limit' (case-insensitive) plus existing CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS. No new module.
Terminal-failure disposition = markConversationEnded (status 'ended'), NOT delete — repo invariant: conversations are never deleted, only archived (conversations.ts:2604-2606).

hazards.summary: The detector must match the ACTUAL runModelSummary rejection message. On code!==0 it rejects with `Summary generation failed: <stderr|stdout>`, and for an oversized prompt the detail is the claude -p JSON envelope containing "result":"Prompt is too long" and "terminal_reason":"blocking_limit". runModelSummaryPromise does NOT inspect is_error (smart-compaction.ts:753-766) — overflow surfaces only via the non-zero exit. If detector patterns drift from this, fix #1 silently no-ops.
hazards.mitigation: Bead-1 unit test asserts isContextOverflowError returns true for the exact issue string; bead-2 test mocks runModelSummary to reject with that real-world message and asserts the truncate-retry fires.

hazards.summary: Even after truncating the chunk, an accumulated <previous-summary> (generateSummaryFromPrompt:805-809) can itself overflow the window.
hazards.mitigation: Bead-2 retry caps BOTH the serialized chunk and previousSummary to a head+tail ceiling; if the retried call still overflows, throw (caller's heuristic fallback in bead-3 catches it).

hazards.summary: Fake-timers repo rule: any test touching retry/backoff/delay must use vi.useFakeTimers(), never real setTimeout. runModelSummary uses a real setTimeout timeout.
hazards.mitigation: Tests mock runModelSummary/generateSummaryForFork entirely so no real LLM spawn or timeout runs; no wall-clock waits.

hazards.summary: Skills↔CLI lockstep: bead-5 must edit src/cli/index.ts handoff help AND sync-sources/skills/pan-handoff/SKILL.md in the same commit or scripts/lint-skills.sh (in npm run lint) fails CI.
hazards.mitigation: Bead-5 scope explicitly includes both files; AC requires npm run lint to pass.

hazards.summary: conversations.ts is dashboard-server-reachable: no execSync, async only. markConversationEnded is a sync DB write (fine).
hazards.mitigation: Reuse existing sync DB helpers (markConversationEnded, updateForkStatus); add no blocking calls.
